<|13.68|> the bread that will allow them to live forever?<|16.92|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.08|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Or maybe it's just someone for whom it's been a very long time since they tasted and saw that the Lord is good.<|7.82|>
<|9.06|> Perhaps they've simply forgotten.<|10.90|>
<|13.08|> Bishop Curry has been pushing us out of our comfort zone since the day he became presiding bishop.<|19.26|>
<|19.92|> He has been preaching here and abroad about evangelism.<|25.90|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> He's been holding revivals.<|3.20|>
<|4.34|> He and the Spirit have been stirring things up.<|7.78|>
<|9.28|> So with Bishop Curry's urging,<|11.86|>
<|13.08|> does it seem a bit selfish to keep all of this to ourselves?<|18.38|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I'm well aware that as Episcopalians, many of us like to get down on our knees, close our eyes really tightly, and pray quietly.<|10.92|>
<|12.14|> As Episcopalians, we like to gloss over passages like the Great Commission at the end of Matthew,<|18.58|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And assume Jesus must have been talking to someone else when he commanded his followers to go out and make disciples.<|7.74|>
<|8.92|> Surely he did not mean us.<|11.26|>
<|12.18|> Perhaps the Baptists.<|13.76|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Yes.<|1.94|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> So I wonder what it would be like in these waning weeks of summer, in our prayer time,<|6.86|>
<|6.86|> as we are making our petitions, counting our blessings, and interceding on the behalf of others,<|13.74|>
<|14.48|> to actually pray for God to put the name of someone on your heart,<|18.82|>
<|19.52|> the name of someone who could also find everlasting life at this altar,<|25.38|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> the name of someone who could be part of our or another family of faith<|6.34|>
<|6.34|> and joyfully feast on the bread of life every week, possibly right here alongside us.<|14.04|>
<|15.44|> I promise I'm not suggesting that you be an evangelist with a capital E.<|20.30|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.48|> I'm asking you to pray about being invitational with a capital I.<|5.76|>
<|6.68|> Who knows what could happen for them and for us?<|11.42|>
<|12.42|> Because really, why can't the wafer be just a little delicious?<|18.44|>
<|19.58|> And why can't we taste and see that the Lord is good?<|23.96|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|>
